description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a rounded bowl and an attached cistern at the back. The toilet is predominantly white but shows visible signs of wear, grime, and discoloration, especially around the base and the inner bowl. The seat and lid are both present, with the lid raised and the seat in place, attached by two visible hinges. The cistern is rectangular and sits directly above the bowl, with no flush lever or button visible from any angle. The base is wide and sturdy, designed to be mounted on the floor, and the overall form is utilitarian, suitable for public or private urban restrooms. The surface appears hard and mostly smooth, though dirt and stains give it a slightly roughened, matte appearance.
description: A worn, white floor-mounted toilet with a rounded bowl, attached cistern, and visible seat and lid. The surface shows grime and discoloration, giving it a used, urban look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat and lid, with visible grime inside the bowl and at the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, seat, and cistern, with visible dirt along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the plumbing connection, with minimal detail on the bowl.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the seat, lid, and cistern, with grime at the base and bowl edge.
